episode.title: Morning Drops #39 - Moving Meditation
episode.description: An episode inspired by how travel helps us see more clearly. Stepping into new places opens the mind, shifts perspective. The music moves through balearic warmth, boogie grooves, world and latin-folk influences, and touches of new wave — a selection built to follow the quiet changes that happen when you're out of your usual rhythm.
songs: España Circo Este - Dormo poco e sogno molto

queue.fact_text: On April 14, 2016's Kumamoto foreshock and the later mainshock also rippled through Kumamoto city itself—a designated city on Japan's Kyushu island, known for its hilltop castle and historic gardens. Overnight, centuries-old stone walls and wooden roofs had to coexist with a freshly rewritten seismic history.

episode.title: Morning Drops #46 - Fog and Fire
episode.description: In today’s Morning Drops, Gaia brings us on a passport-free trip between two Italian energies: the foggy stillness of the North and the volcanic pulse of the South. Throughout the journey, Gaia shares four of her favourite Italian songwriters. Grab your coffee and tune into the radio show from another planet!
songs: Edoardo Bennato - La Torre Di Babele
Lucio Dalla - Com'è Profondo il Mare

queue.match_reason: A city balancing quiet beauty with volcanic energy and sudden earthquakes connects with an episode about contrasting regional energies—fog versus volcanic fire—and how places hold multiple moods at once.

published.fact_text: On April 14, 1958, Sputnik 2—humanity's second artificial satellite and the first to carry a living animal—finally fell back to Earth after 162 days in orbit. Inside had been Laika, a stray dog from Moscow who became an unwitting pioneer of space biology and a symbol of the ethical questions behind early spaceflight.

episode.title: Morning Drops #03 - Space Shift
episode.description: Gaia invites you to shift your mindset with a simple yet powerful reflection: What if we saw emptiness not as a void but as space to create? An energising and feel-good selection of tracks, the perfect way to ease into your day and set the tone for the week ahead.
songs: Willy J Healey - Woke Up Smiling
The Strokes - You Only Live Once

published.match_reason: A story of early space exploration and orbital emptiness pairs naturally with an episode built around reimagining 'space' as possibility, both literally and metaphorically.

published.fact_text: On April 14, 2023, the European Space Agency launched the Jupiter Icy Moons Explorer (JUICE), a spacecraft destined to study the hidden oceans of Europa, Ganymede, and Callisto. Its mission is to probe worlds where liquid water may lurk beneath ice, expanding our search for life far from Earth's warmth.

episode.title: Evening Flows #01 - Yasmin - Crescendo in the Medina
episode.description: The first Evening Flow episode comes from Yasmin. It starts deep in the medina, slow, mysterious, textured, and grows into a liberating dance across sub-Saharan landscapes. A journey through ambient soundscapes, Arabic melodies, Afro-Latin rhythms, and funky highlife grooves.
songs: David Visan & Carlos Campos - Crystal Heart
Evan Mars - Ya Zareef Al Tool
Dele Sosimi & Medlar - You No Fit Touch Am (Medlar Remix)

published.match_reason: A deep-space voyage to distant moons fits with an evening mix that opens in mystery and slowly expands into a wide, exploratory soundscape, like cruising through the outer Solar System.

published.fact_text: On April 14, 1981, Space Shuttle Columbia completed STS-1, the first orbital flight of NASA's partially reusable spaceplane. It was the first time a crewed spacecraft had been launched on a brand-new vehicle and then glided back to land on a runway like an aircraft.

episode.title: Morning Drops #31 - A Trip-Hop Journey
episode.description: Hosted by friend Lorenzo, a journey through the roots and evolution of trip-hop. From Bristol's early experiments with Massive Attack and Tricky, to DJ Shadow's cinematic sampling in California, and Thievery Corporation's global downtempo grooves in Washington D.C. — exploring how this genre blurred boundaries and reshaped electronic music.
songs: Tricky - Hell Is Round the Corner
DJ Shadow - Midnight in a Perfect World

published.match_reason: Columbia blurred the line between rocket and airplane just as trip‑hop blurred lines between genres, both marking an experimental new era in how humans travel—through space or through sound.

published.fact_text: On April 14, 2003, scientists announced the completion of the Human Genome Project, reporting a draft with 99% of the human genetic code sequenced to 99.99% accuracy. In just over a decade, humanity went from guessing at our DNA to reading the instruction book for our own species.

episode.title: Morning Drops #42 - The Joy Of Learning
episode.description:
Hosted by Yasmin, this Morning Drop explores learning as a lifelong process, focusing on persistence, repetition, and the emotional landscape that comes with challenging oneself over time.

Through personal stories and scientific insights, the episode looks at how learning reshapes the brain, generates joy through effort, and why progress often arrives after confusion and frustration. It reflects on the so-called delayed pleasure effect, the role of dopamine in motivation, and how curiosity and learning support long-term wellbeing.

The episode also questions our cultural relationship with failure and invites a softer, more realistic approach to learning, one that accepts mistakes as part of growth and sees persistence as a quiet form of strength.

Mood: reflective, grounded, quietly hopeful
Music genre: alternative rock, hip hop, electronic, neo-soul, drill

A space to listen slowly and stay with the process.

published.match_reason: Decoding the human genome is a monumental act of learning and persistence, perfectly aligned with an episode centered on the science and emotion of long-term learning and discovery.

published.fact_text: On April 14, 1935, the "Black Sunday" dust storm roared across the Great Plains, turning midday into midnight as walls of soil blotted out the sun. It became an iconic moment of the Dust Bowl, showing how human farming practices and drought could transform rich prairie into a sky-darkening storm.

episode.title: Morning Drops #16 - The Sound of Tuareg
episode.description: A journey into the sound of the Tuareg: from traditional chants and desert rituals to the electrified rhythms of exile and resistance. Music shaped by sand, memory, and survival.

published.match_reason: A story of dust, drought, and survival in harsh landscapes resonates with Tuareg desert blues, music literally born from life shaped by sand and resilience.

published.fact_text: On April 14, 1986, the heaviest hailstones ever recorded—each about the weight of a household brick, roughly 1 kilogram—fell on Gopalganj District in Bangladesh. These ice boulders crashing from the sky tragically killed 92 people and remain a stark reminder of the extremes hidden in Earth's weather.

episode.title: Morning Drops #45 - It Can't Rain Forever
episode.description:
In this first broadcast of 2026, Gaia returns from the vivid landscapes of Morocco to open a space for reflection. This episode navigates the delicate transition between closing a significant chapter and stepping into the new year. It explores the concept of constancy amidst change, celebrating the grounding force of friendship and the creative sanctuary that is Sedna FM.

The musical selection mirrors this journey, moving from the orchestral depth of changing seasons into the golden warmth of psychedelic soul. It recalls memories of Swiss mountains and unexpected downpours, reminding us that shared optimism can weather any storm.

Take a moment to breathe in the new morning and listen deeply.

Mood: Reflective, Warm, Transitional, Hopeful
Music genre: Classic Rock, Psychedelic Soul, Pop Rock, Soft Rock

published.match_reason: A record-shattering hailstorm meets an episode themed around rain, storms, and weathering difficult seasons, both touching on nature's intensity and human hope.

published.fact_text: On April 14, 1999, a ferocious hailstorm struck Sydney, Australia, hurling ice stones the size of cricket balls and shredding rooftops, cars, and glass. With insured damage reaching A$2.3 billion, it became the costliest natural disaster in Australian history at the time.

episode.title: Morning Drops #05 - Electric Shock
episode.description: An energizing episode with electrifying sounds to wake you up and get you moving. High energy tracks that deliver a jolt of musical electricity to start your day.

published.match_reason: A violent storm that electrified the sky and city aligns with an "Electric Shock" set full of high-voltage guitars and sudden impact.

published.fact_text: On April 14, 2024, unusual, intense flooding swept across parts of the Persian Gulf region, killing at least 19 people in Oman. In some areas, normally dry wadis turned into raging torrents within hours, revealing how fragile desert cities can be when climate and weather shift abruptly.

episode.title: Morning Drops #33 - Immerse
episode.description: An analogy between diving, one of Gaia's great passions, and the act of immersing in life itself. A reflection on what happens when we go below the surface: when we let ourselves be fully present. From moments of calm suspension to warm, rhythmic flow, the selection moves like water — fluid, organic, and alive. An invitation to slow down, listen deeper, and float.
songs: Pino D'Angiò - Un Po' d'Uva e un Liquore

published.match_reason: A powerful, destructive immersion in floodwater contrasts yet connects with an episode about choosing how we immerse ourselves in water and experience, turning a harsh reality into a contemplative echo.

published.fact_text: On April 14, 2016, a strong foreshock struck Kumamoto, Japan, shaking castles, temples, and hillside towns in Kyushu. It was only the prelude to an even larger earthquake that would follow, a reminder that some of Earth's most dramatic events arrive in ominous whispers before the roar.

episode.title: Morning Drops #43 - Looking Back
episode.description:
In this first Morning Drop of 2026, Yasmin opens a space for quiet reflection on the nature of transitions. The episode traces a personal journey that began three years ago, when a sudden injury on New Year’s Day turned a desire for movement into a mandatory season of stillness.

Through the story of a broken knee and the slow process of relearning to walk, the narrative explores the difference between being strong and being aware. It touches on the vulnerability of the body, the unexpected joy found in daily progress, and the warmth that comes from finally allowing oneself to accept help. The musical selection mirrors this path of recovery, moving from the uncertainty of change to the steady rhythm of healing.

Mood: Reflective, Resilient, Grounded
Music genre: Indie Pop, Cinematic, World, Soul

We invite you to listen deeply and find comfort in your own pace.
songs: Kelly Finnigan - I Don't Wanna Wait
Goran Bregović & Iggy Pop - This Is a Film

published.match_reason: A foreshock before a major quake mirrors the theme of unexpected rupture and gradual recovery, echoing an episode about sudden physical change and slow healing.

published.fact_text: On April 14, 1895, Ljubljana was rocked by its most destructive earthquake on record, damaging over 10,000 buildings. The disaster unexpectedly triggered a wave of urban renewal, helping shape the modern cityscape that now blends Art Nouveau architecture with seismic memory.

episode.title: Morning Drops #20 - Hitchhiking Poland
episode.description: Yasmin takes you back to the summer of 2020, when borders were still uncertain and a long-held wish to visit Poland finally came true. Through four tracks, tracing her journey from a stranger's couch in Poznań to the peaks of Zakopane.

published.match_reason: A Central European city rebuilt after shaking pairs with a travelogue across nearby Central/Eastern Europe, both touching on how places evolve through upheaval and movement.

published.fact_text: On April 14, 1894, the world's first commercial motion picture house opened in New York City, offering short films through ten coin-operated Kinetoscopes. People paid to peer individually into wooden cabinets, like mechanical peep-holes into the future of cinema.

episode.title: Morning Drops #54 - Intentional Listening
episode.description:
Do you remember what it felt like to listen to music without a streaming platform?
In this Morning Drops episode, Gaia reflects on a recent experiment: stepping away from algorithmic listening and going back to an iPod. 

What starts as a small personal choice opens into a wider meditation on attention, presence, and what happens when music is no longer treated as something infinite, but as something we choose with care.

Tracklist:
Scientist - Drum Song Dub
The Other People Place - Let Me Be Me
A Forest Mighty Black - Fresh In My Mind
Cymande - Bra
songs: The Other People Place - Let Me Be Me
A Forest Mighty Black - Fresh In My Mind

published.match_reason: Peering into individual Kinetoscopes to watch films echoes the idea of deliberate, device-shaped ways of consuming art that this episode revisits through intentional, non-algorithmic listening.

published.fact_text: On April 14, 1929, roaring engines echoed through the narrow streets of the Principality of Monaco as the inaugural Monaco Grand Prix took place. William Grover-Williams, racing in a dark green Bugatti Type 35, won the glamorous new event that would become a crown jewel of motorsport.

episode.title: Sedna FM #05 - On The Go - Zero Zero
episode.description: An energetic mix for when you're on the move. Zero Zero captures that feeling of starting fresh, moving forward, and embracing the journey. Perfect for travel, walks, or just getting things done.
songs: Grace Jones - Pull up to the bumper
Easy star all star - Soul love
James Brown - Talking loud and saying nothing (remix)

published.match_reason: High-speed racing through city streets matches an "on the move" playlist built for motion, momentum, and the thrill of the journey.

published.fact_text: On April 14, 1775, in Philadelphia, Benjamin Franklin, Benjamin Rush, and fellow reformers organized the Society for the Relief of Free Negroes Unlawfully Held in Bondage. It became the first abolition society in North America, quietly planting seeds for the long fight against slavery.

episode.title: Morning Drops #08 - (un)Boxing
episode.description: An episode about breaking free from expectations and labels. Music that helps us unbox ourselves and embrace who we truly are.
songs: Kendrick Lamar - The Blacker the Berry

published.match_reason: An early abolition society dedicated to human freedom pairs with an episode about breaking out of boxes and oppressive labels, amplified by politically conscious hip‑hop.

published.fact_text: On April 14, 1988, in Geneva, the Soviet Union signed agreements at the United Nations committing to withdraw its troops from Afghanistan. After nearly a decade of conflict, it marked the beginning of the end of one of the Cold War’s most grinding proxy wars.

episode.title: Morning Drops #44 - Residente
episode.description:
In this edition of Morning Drops, Yasmin navigates the complex geography of René Pérez Joglar, known to the world as Residente. His story is not just a musical evolution; it is a timeline marked by resistance, lyrical obsession, and a relentless search for identity.

From the gated streets of Trujillo Alto to the global stage, we explore how a restless mind transformed into the voice of a generation. The selection moves through the textures of his life: hearing the raw energy of his early rebellion, the sharp precision of his political critique, and finally, the fragile silence of his most personal confessions.

It is a study in duality. We witness the rapper who dismantles giants and the son who misses his mother. It is a soundscape that proves that sometimes, the loudest revolution happens inside.

Mood: Raw, Revolutionary, Introspective, Honest
Music genre: Latin Alternative, Hip Hop, Urban, World

published.match_reason: A geopolitical step toward ending a war fits with an episode centered on politically charged, anti-imperialist lyrics and the personal cost of conflict and resistance.

published.fact_text: On April 14, 1988, the U.S. Navy frigate USS Samuel B. Roberts struck an Iranian naval mine in the Persian Gulf during Operation Earnest Will. The blast tore open the hull and nearly sank the ship, but the crew's desperate damage-control efforts kept her afloat against the odds.

episode.title: Morning Drops #49 - Rage Against The Machine
episode.description:
In this Morning Drop, Yasmin revisits the story of Rage Against the Machine — a band born in tension, shaped by conviction, and remembered for moments that blurred the line between stage and protest.
From early Los Angeles unrest to Wall Street performances, this episode moves through rupture, resistance, and legacy — carried by songs that still feel urgent today.
Listen closely.
songs: Rage Against the Machine - Killing in the Name
Rage Against the Machine - Without a Face
Rage Against the Machine - Guerrilla Radio
Rage Against the Machine - Sleep Now in the Fire

published.match_reason: A warship torn open by a mine in a tense geopolitical standoff aligns with the explosive, anti-war, anti-imperialist fury of Rage Against the Machine.

published.fact_text: On April 14, 1994, during Operation Provide Comfort in northern Iraq, two U.S. Air Force F-15s mistakenly shot down two U.S. Army Black Hawk helicopters. The friendly fire incident killed 26 people and exposed how even high-tech identification systems can fail in the fog of modern war.

episode.title: Morning Drops #52- Think Again
episode.description:
In this Morning Drop, Yasmin explores the quiet art of changing your mind, guided by Adam Grant's book Think Again. From the beliefs we carry without questioning to the identities we build around what we think we know, this episode follows the slow, sometimes uncomfortable process of unlearning.

Along the way, we meet a Serbian trumpet player who had to let go of one dream to find another, a Tunisian oud master who challenged centuries of tradition from the inside, and a Japanese band who found freedom in playing for no one. The music moves from Balkan brass to Mediterranean stillness to psychedelic drift, tracing the shape of a mind opening up.

Listen slowly, and ask yourself: is there something you believe today that deserves a second look?

Tracklist:
Boban Marković Orkestar – SAT
Anouar Brahem – The Astounding Eyes of Rita
Kikagaku Moyo – Dripping Sun
Son Lux – Lost It to Trying
songs: Anouar Brahem – The Astounding Eyes of Rita
Son Lux – Lost It to Trying

published.match_reason: A tragic misidentification in combat speaks to the dangers of unquestioned assumptions, pairing well with an episode about rethinking, unlearning, and examining what we think we know.

published.fact_text: On April 14, 1912, the British liner RMS Titanic scraped along an iceberg in the North Atlantic, opening a jagged wound in her hull below the waterline. The "unsinkable" ship's slow, fatal flooding would soon become one of the most infamous maritime disasters in history.

episode.title: Morning Drops #40 - Diving With Orcas
episode.description: Yasmin's friend Caroline takes us beyond the polar circle, into the Arctic waters of Norway, where orcas and humpback whales gather each winter. A personal journey shaped by a quiet obsession with orcas, from Patagonia to the icy fjords of the north. The soundscape moves gently between cinematic swells and soft, rhythmic pulses — echoing the cold light of Arctic mornings and the grace of underwater encounters.

published.match_reason: A North Atlantic shipwreck and icy waters resonate with an episode set in cold oceans among whales and orcas, both filled with cinematic, sea‑soaked imagery.

published.fact_text: On April 14, 1561, citizens of Nuremberg reported a strange celestial phenomenon: shapes like spheres, cylinders, and crosses filling the morning sky, as if engaged in an aerial battle. Whether optical illusion, atmospheric event, or early mass UFO report, the woodcut that recorded it remains one of history’s most peculiar sky stories.

episode.title: Morning Drops #48 - Cosmic Monday
episode.description:
Cosmic Monday is a Morning Drops episode built around four Italian dance records, tracing how disco rhythms slowly became electronic language.
Gaia moves through four tracks like chapters: groove first, then machines, then that bright, futuristic feeling you only get when the bassline is doing the talking. It's not nostalgia, exactly. It's more like returning to a sound that still works — especially on mornings when you need a little extra energy, but you don't want anything aggressive.
Take it as a slow transmission. Let it carry you into the day.
songs: Giovanotti Mondani Meccanici - Don't Ask Me Why

published.match_reason: An eerie 16th‑century "cosmic" sky battle aligns with a cosmic‑themed electronic dance selection, turning historical celestial strangeness into a playful space‑disco transmission.

published.fact_text: On April 14, 1940, British Royal Marines landed at the Norwegian town of Namsos as part of an Allied attempt to counter the German invasion of Norway. Their arrival marked the start of a difficult campaign fought among fjords, forests, and snow-covered valleys.

episode.title: Morning Drops #31 - Sailing With Germans
episode.description: From the deck of a sailing boat, hosted by Yasmin and her special guests. A week at sea with six Germans, each with their own stories, songs, and ways of moving through the waves. From shared meals to unexpected laughter, from quiet resilience to sun-soaked joy — a collection of voices and tracks that reflect what it means to journey together.
songs: Ben Howard - Keep Your Head Up

published.match_reason: A World War II naval landing in Norwegian waters is reimagined through a peaceful sailing trip with German friends, keeping the maritime setting while replacing conflict with connection.

published.fact_text: On April 14, 1997, 16-year-old Pai Hsiao-yen, daughter of Taiwanese entertainer Pai Bing-bing, was kidnapped on her way to school in Taipei. The crime, which ended in her murder, shocked Taiwan, dominated media coverage, and spurred intense debate over policing, sensationalism, and celebrity privacy.

episode.title: Morning Drops #19 - Wabi Sabi
episode.description: Wabi-sabi is a story about a handmade mug, a small mistake, and the idea that imperfection can be something to keep—not correct. In this Morning Drop, Gaia shares a moment that didn’t go to plan, and four tracks that echo the same spirit: rough takes, studio accidents, lo-fi textures, and quiet surprises. A soft, thoughtful selection moving between warm electronics, classic riffs, and raw edges.A reminder that not everything needs to be polished. Sometimes, it’s better that way.

published.match_reason: A deeply painful, media-saturated crime tied to a public figure pairs with a gentle episode about imperfection and things not going to plan, offering a softer, reflective counterpoint to a story of loss and scrutiny.

published.fact_text: On April 14, 2023's cousin in history—April 14, 2023 also stands as a marker of European cooperation in space through ESA's JUICE mission—highlighting how 22 countries pool their resources to chase icy moons hundreds of millions of kilometers away. It's a reminder that exploration is often a collective act, stitched together across borders and decades.

episode.title: Sedna FM #02 - Destination Sedna
episode.description: Continuing the Sedna FM journey together with family and friends! Episode two of the main series — exploring new musical territories and deepening the connection with our listeners. A collective voyage through sound.

published.match_reason: A pan-European space agency sending a probe to distant worlds aligns with a collaborative episode framed as a shared voyage, both about collective journeys into the unknown.

published.fact_text: On April 14, 1958's space dog Laika also stands for all Soviet space dogs, part of a program that sent at least 36 dogs into space to test rockets and life support. Many never returned, but their journeys quietly paved the way for human cosmonauts who followed.

episode.title: Sedna FM #01 - November Bomb
